sections.introduction:
You are at your strongest when the field gets cleaner, not louder. A blade that cuts noise away is the right image for this phase.

The central axis right now is career, and the operating bias is career: Staged commit. A scene where clutter gets stripped from a crowded stage is where your clarity and clean cuts become visible.

At around age 31, the active 10-year cycle is 31-40 (乙亥 (wood)). This phase rewards sequence more than speed.

Split decisions and lock scope, deadline, and ownership in writing. Key grounding comes from day master metal, jeongjae frame, useful element fire, dominant western element air.
sections.personalityDeep:
Your baseline temperament is strongest when standards are visible and pace is controlled. The upside of this structure is how quickly clarity and clean cuts can be established once the room is clean.

The risk appears when dulling the edge by deciding too fast turns into premature certainty before the shape of the situation is fully clear. In practical terms, you work best when the standard is named first and momentum is allowed to follow later.

This personality is less about dramatic speed and more about knowing where the line should be drawn before anything becomes final. Key grounding comes from day master metal, jeongjae frame, useful element fire, dominant western element air.
sections.careerPath: Your career gets sharper when the scene gets cleaner. A blade that cuts noise away is the right image here. The real edge is not doing more, but deciding what you will be measured by. The live career scene is learning acceleration. Dulling the edge by deciding too fast is the work risk to watch. In career, the better move is not a one-shot close but a staged path: lock the role first, then insert a review point before final commitment. Key grounding comes from windfall signal, Daeun metal.
sections.relationshipDynamics: Relationships improve here when interpretation, boundaries, and pacing line up before commitment pressure rises. This phase is less about forcing a conclusion and more about matching distance, pace, and expectations. A blade that cuts noise away shows up in relationships as the need to draw the line before going deeper. Pushing commitment or crossing the line without enough verification is more likely to enlarge misunderstanding. Key grounding comes from strong synergy, hard clash.
sections.wealthPotential: Wealth lasts longer when numbers, limits, and terms stay clean. A blade that cuts noise away is the right money image here. Wealth is running in a mixed phase where upside and risk activate together, so reality is more likely to form through staged verification than direct commitment. A scene where clutter gets stripped from a crowded stage is where the money decision becomes visible: decide what gets resources and what gets cut before you scale anything. Dulling the edge by deciding too fast tends to show up in money as rushed commitments, vague terms, and downside that was never defined in writing. The real money edge now is not bigger upside, but knowing exactly what can leak, what remains negotiable, and where the downside stops. Before you chase more money, lock the terms, settlement logic, and loss boundary. Key grounding comes from windfall signal, Daeun metal.
sections.healthGuidance: Health depends more on recovery rhythm and overload detection than on endurance alone. This phase rewards repeatable recovery before intensity. Dulling the edge by deciding too fast often turns into fatigue build-up or delayed recovery in the body. Key grounding comes from nurturing mode, Jupiter-Saturn square.
sections.lifeMission: This chapter is about learning how to carry a blade that cuts noise away for the long run without losing its edge. The previous 21-30 cycle set the habits you are now either carrying forward or editing. The next 41-50 cycle is likely to shift emphasis toward 甲戌 (wood), so this period should be used to prepare that handoff. The current life chapter is centered on career, and that is where the main lesson is becoming visible. The long arc improves less through isolated wins and more through standards you can repeat under pressure. The scenes pushing this life chapter forward are learning acceleration, route recheck, commute restructure. dulling the edge by deciding too fast is the long-range failure point, especially when standards collapse or commitments are rushed. Key grounding comes from peak harmony, Shinsal noble support.
sections.timingAdvice: This is a usable now window for career, but only if sequence comes before speed. The timing edge here is not acceleration, but knowing when clarity and clean cuts should actually be used. The clean version of this move is simple: write down scope, deadline, and ownership before anything becomes public. Pause the move when assumptions start replacing direct communication or when scope cannot be verified in writing. Key grounding comes from astro progressions, relationship three-branch harmony.
sections.actionPlan: The current priority is career: Staged commit. Start by fixing the role, the scope, and the review point before you try to close anything. Do not mistake momentum for readiness; if the plan cannot survive one round of review, it is not ready to close. Before moving, make sure the next step can still be verified, revised, or paused without creating unnecessary damage. Key grounding comes from authority maturity stage, Daeun metal.
sections.conclusion: The conclusion is simple: in career, the person who sets the standard first usually controls the phase. What changes the outcome here is not raw talent, but how clarity and clean cuts get applied in sequence. Key grounding comes from saju fiveElements, snapshot.

renderedMarkdown:
## Section: introduction

You are at your strongest when the field gets cleaner, not louder. A blade that cuts noise away is the right image for this phase.

The central axis right now is career, and the operating bias is career: Staged commit. A scene where clutter gets stripped from a crowded stage is where your clarity and clean cuts become visible.

At around age 31, the active 10-year cycle is 31-40 (乙亥 (wood)). This phase rewards sequence more than speed.

Split decisions and lock scope, deadline, and ownership in writing.

Evidence: day master metal, jeongjae frame, useful element fire, dominant western element air

## Section: personalityDeep

Your baseline temperament is strongest when standards are visible and pace is controlled. The upside of this structure is how quickly clarity and clean cuts can be established once the room is clean.

The risk appears when dulling the edge by deciding too fast turns into premature certainty before the shape of the situation is fully clear. In practical terms, you work best when the standard is named first and momentum is allowed to follow later.

This personality is less about dramatic speed and more about knowing where the line should be drawn before anything becomes final.

Evidence: day master metal, jeongjae frame, useful element fire, dominant western element air

## Section: careerPath

Your career gets sharper when the scene gets cleaner. A blade that cuts noise away is the right image here. The real edge is not doing more, but deciding what you will be measured by. The live career scene is learning acceleration. Dulling the edge by deciding too fast is the work risk to watch. In career, the better move is not a one-shot close but a staged path: lock the role first, then insert a review point before final commitment.

Evidence: windfall signal, Daeun metal

## Section: relationshipDynamics

Relationships improve here when interpretation, boundaries, and pacing line up before commitment pressure rises. This phase is less about forcing a conclusion and more about matching distance, pace, and expectations. A blade that cuts noise away shows up in relationships as the need to draw the line before going deeper. Pushing commitment or crossing the line without enough verification is more likely to enlarge misunderstanding.

Evidence: strong synergy, hard clash

## Section: wealthPotential

Wealth lasts longer when numbers, limits, and terms stay clean. A blade that cuts noise away is the right money image here. Wealth is running in a mixed phase where upside and risk activate together, so reality is more likely to form through staged verification than direct commitment. A scene where clutter gets stripped from a crowded stage is where the money decision becomes visible: decide what gets resources and what gets cut before you scale anything. Dulling the edge by deciding too fast tends to show up in money as rushed commitments, vague terms, and downside that was never defined in writing. The real money edge now is not bigger upside, but knowing exactly what can leak, what remains negotiable, and where the downside stops. Before you chase more money, lock the terms, settlement logic, and loss boundary.

Evidence: windfall signal, Daeun metal

## Section: healthGuidance

Health depends more on recovery rhythm and overload detection than on endurance alone. This phase rewards repeatable recovery before intensity. Dulling the edge by deciding too fast often turns into fatigue build-up or delayed recovery in the body.

Evidence: nurturing mode, Jupiter-Saturn square

## Section: lifeMission

This chapter is about learning how to carry a blade that cuts noise away for the long run without losing its edge. The previous 21-30 cycle set the habits you are now either carrying forward or editing. The next 41-50 cycle is likely to shift emphasis toward 甲戌 (wood), so this period should be used to prepare that handoff. The current life chapter is centered on career, and that is where the main lesson is becoming visible. The long arc improves less through isolated wins and more through standards you can repeat under pressure. The scenes pushing this life chapter forward are learning acceleration, route recheck, commute restructure. dulling the edge by deciding too fast is the long-range failure point, especially when standards collapse or commitments are rushed.

Evidence: peak harmony, Shinsal noble support

## Section: timingAdvice

This is a usable now window for career, but only if sequence comes before speed. The timing edge here is not acceleration, but knowing when clarity and clean cuts should actually be used. The clean version of this move is simple: write down scope, deadline, and ownership before anything becomes public. Pause the move when assumptions start replacing direct communication or when scope cannot be verified in writing.

Evidence: astro progressions, relationship three-branch harmony

## Section: actionPlan

The current priority is career: Staged commit. Start by fixing the role, the scope, and the review point before you try to close anything. Do not mistake momentum for readiness; if the plan cannot survive one round of review, it is not ready to close. Before moving, make sure the next step can still be verified, revised, or paused without creating unnecessary damage.

Evidence: authority maturity stage, Daeun metal

## Section: conclusion

The conclusion is simple: in career, the person who sets the standard first usually controls the phase. What changes the outcome here is not raw talent, but how clarity and clean cuts get applied in sequence.

Evidence: saju fiveElements, snapshot
